title: Function in generate block results in "Broken link in node" crash
body:
Can you please attach an example that shows the issue or missing feature?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs)

```verilog
module top(out);
   output logic out;

   generate
      if (1) begin : defs
         function foo;
            foo = '1;
         endfunction
      end else begin : defs
         function foo;
            foo = '0;
         endfunction
      end
   endgenerate

   assign out = defs.foo();
endmodule
```

What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  (Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)

```
%Error: Internal Error: top.v:16:22: ../V3Broken.cpp:182: Broken link in node (or something without maybePointedTo): 'm_taskp && !m_taskp->brokeExists()' @ ./V3Ast__gen_impl.h:5861
                                   : ... note: In instance 'top'
-node: FUNCREF 0x555556b63c00 <e1249> {e16aw} @dt=0@  foo -> .=defs FUNC 0x555556b62bb0 <e1273> {e10at} @dt=0@  foo [VFUNC]
   16 |    assign out = defs.foo();
      |                      ^~~
                        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.047 for more assistance.
```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

`verilator --cc --debug top.v`

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?

`Verilator 5.047 devel rev v5.046-75-ga7a5c9f54`

What OS and distribution are you using?

Ubuntu 20.04.1 LTS

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Yes.
